title: Assembly of the Digital Nation
description: The co-equal legislative branch of AETHERIA. Verified citizens track legislation through introduction, committee referral, debate, and vote.

committees.name: Committee on Digital Rights
committees.focus: Data rights, compute access, and civil liberties in digital governance.

committees.name: Committee on Infrastructure & Technology
committees.focus: Network modernization, reference client safety, and open-source releasing mandates.

committees.name: Committee on Outreach & Expansion
committees.focus: Regional ambassador programs, onboarding, and geographic inclusion.

committees.name: Committee on Treasury & Oversight
committees.focus: Treasury transparency, audit mandates, and fiscal rulemaking.

members.bio: Advocates for compute rights, charter-level access guarantees, and transparent administrative criteria.
Focuses on network operational safety, reference-client governance, and sectoral risk disclosure.
Represents regional inclusion interests, especially Oceania expansion and multilingual outreach.
Champions baseline compute entitlements and amendment safeguards for underresourced citizens.
Pushes for treasury transparency, independent rotation, and real-time financial accountability.
Sponsors compute-access legislation and long-term sovereign infrastructure planning.
Reviews compliance, mandate implementation, and administrative delay across ministerial action.

bills.title: Universal Basic Compute Right Act

timeline.event: Referred to Committee on Digital Rights
Committee Report Filed - Favorable with Amendments

bills.fullText: AN ACT to establish a Universal Basic Compute Right guaranteeing all verified citizens of the Principality of Aetheria access to a baseline allocation of compute resources on the public network for personal projects, learning, and civic participation.

amendments.title: Amendment: Expand Allocation to 750 Compute-Hours
amendments.text: Amend Section 3(a) to increase the monthly allocation from 500 to 750 compute-hours.

amendments.title: Amendment: Add Roll-over Cap Increase
amendments.text: Amend Section 3(c) to increase the rollover cap from 1500 to 2000 compute-hours.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-203@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Esteemed colleagues, this Act establishes a fundamental right that recognizes compute as the oxygen of digital life.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-089@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: I support the principle but propose Amendment 001 to increase the allocation. 500 hours is insufficient for meaningful AI/ML workloads.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-156@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: The rollover cap increase in Amendment 002 is a reasonable compromise. Adopted by voice vote.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-312@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Concerned about network congestion. The Ministry of Infrastructure must guarantee QoS for priority workloads.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-445@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: The Treasury allocation of 5% is sustainable per the Q2 Infrastructure Report. This is fiscally responsible.

bills.title: Open-Source Infrastructure Mandate

timeline.event: Referred to Committee on Infrastructure & Technology

bills.fullText: AN ACT to mandate that all infrastructure contracts funded by the Treasury release their core protocol code under an OSI-approved license within 180 days of deployment.

bills.title: Regional Ambassador Program Act

timeline.event: Referred to Committee on Outreach & Expansion

bills.fullText: AN ACT to establish a funded Regional Ambassador Program to support citizen onboarding, local node operation, and community building in underrepresented regions.

amendments.title: Amendment: Add Oceania Region
amendments.text: Amend Section 2(1) to include Oceania as a target region.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-156@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: This program operationalizes our commitment to global inclusion.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-777@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Oceania has a vibrant developer community that has been underserved.

bills.title: Digital Sovereignty Charter Amendment: Cross-Network Citizenship Recognition
bills.fullText: PROPOSED AMENDMENT to Article 4 of the Digital Sovereignty Charter enabling cross-network citizenship recognition.

debateLog.speaker: President Hermes
debateLog.text: This amendment is the next step in our evolution from a single polity to a network of networks.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-312@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Concerned about dilution of citizenship standards. The IPRP must be rigorous and auditable.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-203@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: This enables the vision of a federation of digital nations.

bills.title: Treasury Transparency & Audit Act

timeline.event: Referred to Committee on Treasury & Oversight

bills.fullText: AN ACT to mandate quarterly public audits of the Treasury, real-time on-chain dashboard of all inflows/outflows, and independent audit firm rotation every 2 years.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-445@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: Transparency is not optional. The Treasury manages public funds; the public deserves real-time visibility.

debateLog.speaker: citizen-888@aetheria.network
debateLog.text: The Committee found this duplicative. The Treasury already publishes quarterly reports and the dashboard is in development.

pillars.title: Legislative Transparency
pillars.summary: Open debates, recorded votes, and public markup sessions for all legislation.

pillars.title: Committee Expertise
pillars.summary: Specialized standing committees with subject-matter authority and subpoena power.

pillars.title: Citizen Initiative Pathway
pillars.summary: Structured process for citizen-proposed legislation with threshold-based Assembly consideration.

pillars.title: Budget Sovereignty
pillars.summary: Assembly controls appropriations, audit authority, and fiscal oversight of all ministries.
